title: I Bootstrapped to *$1M* ARR. Here's What Nobody Tells You
subtitle: Startup lessons

title: Revenue before *funding*
body: Investors respect traction. We closed our first 50 customers before ever writing a pitch deck.

title: Hire *slow*, fire fast
body: Our worst quarter came after a hiring spree. Every bad hire costs 3x their salary in lost productivity and morale.

title: Say *no* to features
body: We killed 80% of our roadmap. The product got simpler, churn dropped 40%, and support tickets halved overnight.

title: Pricing is *positioning*
body: We 3x'd our price and conversions went up. Cheap signals low value. Price for the customer you want to attract.

title: Distribution beats *product*
body: A good product with great distribution wins every time. We spent 60% of our time on channels, not code.

title: What's the *hardest* lesson you've learned building a company?
subtitle: Follow for more startup insights
